steps.title: Use the intercept input
steps.content: The value $c$ is $f(0)$. In exponential form, $f(0)$ is displayed as the coefficient when the exponent becomes $0$ at $x=0$.

steps.title: Check the unshifted form
steps.content:
For $f(x)=33(1.5)^x$, substituting $x=0$ gives:
$$f(0)=33(1.5)^0=33$$
So the value of $c$ is shown directly as the coefficient.

steps.title: Eliminate shifted exponents
steps.content:
The other forms have exponents $x+1$, $x-1$, or $x-2$, so their coefficients represent function values at shifted inputs instead of $x=0$.
The answer is B.
